attractions.name: Kinkaku-ji temple
attractions.description: Also known as the Golden Pavilion, a stunning Zen temple covered in gold leaf

attractions.name: Fushimi Inari Shrine
attractions.description: Thousands of vermillion torii gates form a mesmerizing pathway up the mountain

attractions.name: Fushimi Inari-Taisha
attractions.description: Famous shrine with torii gates

attractions.name: Sannenzaka & Ninenzaka Streets
attractions.description: Charming stone-paved streets lined with traditional houses, cafés, and craft shops

attractions.name: Philosopher's Path
attractions.description: A peaceful canal-side walk lined with cherry blossoms

attractions.name: Amanohashidate
attractions.description: A scenic sandbar in Japan, renowned for its unique landscape. It stretches for about 3.6 kilometers. Said to be one of the 3 most beautiful views in Japan and is often called 'the bridge to heaven'

attractions.name: Ine Funaya
attractions.description: Like the Venice of Japan. The distinctive feature of this village is its houses, which are built directly on the water. Offers a glimpse into the traditional way of life in this coastal region

attractions.name: Ninenzaka & Sannenzaka Streets
attractions.description: Beautifully preserved historic lanes filled with traditional shops and tea houses

attractions.name: Kurama-dera Temple
attractions.description: Serene mountain temple north of Kyoto, revered for its spiritual energy, natural beauty, and as the legendary home of the Tengu spirits

attractions.name: Kurama Onsen
attractions.description: Open-air baths with mountain views for a blissful pause amid nature

attractions.name: Kibune Village
attractions.description: Quaint riverside town known for its shrines, quaint inns, and wooden bridges

attractions.name: Ninomaru Gardens
attractions.description: Beautifully landscaped Edo-period garden featuring ponds, stones, and manicured pine trees, symbolizing harmony and elegance
attractions.entry_fee: Included in Nijo Castle entry (¥1,300 per person)
